raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Barclays Bank Delawares handling of disputed charges and its claimed balance on my AAdvantage Aviator account. Barclays has not provided a lawful, transaction-level explanation of what it says I owe, yet it continues to insist the balance is valid. 

The account history itself shows unresolved errors. The XXXX relationship reflected a {$0.00} statement balance as of XX/XX/XXXX. Later, on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX transactions posted in the amounts of {$6900.00} and {$450.00}. In the XX/XX/XXXX statement cycle, Barclays then posted three major payment reversals totaling {$21000.00} : {$6900.00} reversed as CUSTOMER ADVISES NOT AUTHORIZED, {$7400.00} reversed as INSUFFICIENT FUNDS, and {$6600.00} reversed as INSUFFICIENT FUNDS. That same XX/XX/XXXX cycle also shows {$13000.00} in credits for prior disputed foreign transactions. After that, Barclays continued adding fees and purchase interest. 

Barclays own CFPB responses have not resolved these issues. In one response, Barclays stated that previously identified fraud transactions had already been credited and that the remaining balance was valid. In a later response, Barclays said it would not assist further, relied on alleged verification calls, and asserted that no new or different information had been provided. Barclays still has not produced the underlying evidence needed to support those conclusions.

This is a billing-error dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z. A billing error includes an unauthorized extension of credit, a failure to properly credit a payment, an accounting error, and a request for documentary clarification. Regulation Z also provides that when a consumer properly disputes a billing error, the consumer need not pay, and the creditor may not try to collect, the portion of any required payment the consumer believes is related to the disputed amount, including related finance or other charges. In addition, in any action to enforce liability for credit-card use, the issuer bears the burden of proving that the use was authorized or that the conditions for liability were satisfied. ( eCFR ) My dispute is specific. I dispute the validity and documentary support for the three payment reversals, the fees and interest that flowed from those reversals and the disputed balance, and Barclays unsupported conclusion that the remaining amount is valid. Barclays has not provided the payment trace data, return codes, timestamps, internal logs, call recordings, transcripts, or agent notes that would be needed to verify its position.
